Issue of Share Options
Following the announcement of entering into a loan agreement on 7 April 2026, Synergia Energy Limited (
Synergia
or the

) advises that it has issued 2,169,649,244 share options ("

latest.title: Camelot CCS and Cambay PSC Update
latest.content:
Today 12:00
RNS Number : 5118I
Synergia Energy Ltd
16 June 2026
RNS Announcement
16 June 2026
AIM: SYN
Camelot CCS and Cambay PSC Update
Synergia Energy Limited (
Synergia
or the
Company
) announces the following update regarding its Camelot CCS project in the UK Southern North Sea and its Cambay PSC (WI 50%) in India.
Camelot CCS Project
Synergia has a 50% interest in the CS019 Camelot carbon storage licence in the Southern North Sea. In November 2025, the Company submitted a comprehensive legacy well integrity risk assessment report to the North Sea Transition authority
(NSTA
). The risk assessment report was reviewed on 9 June 2026 during an NSTA-hosted workshop in London.
The CS019 licence contains 13 legacy wells. The detailed abandonment data for two wells (53/01-4 and 53/01-6) have not been able to be located and the NSTA is concerned that the Mobil composite logs produced in the 1970's cannot be corroborated. The wells were abandoned with an open hole section in the Leman Sandstone and Zechstein formations and after circa 50 years, Synergia is confident these hole sections will have collapsed. Consequently, the wells cannot be re-abandoned.
In view of the uncertainty of being able to obtain a storage permit for the Leman Sandstone storage reservoir together with commercial uncertainties, the Company has decided to apply to relinquish its CS019 licence, and anticipates its 50% joint venture partner in the licence, Harbour Energy, to also request a relinquishment of its licence.
Although the Company retains its interest in the CCS sector via its Cambay CCS project in India, going forward, Synergia will continue to focus on the further development of its Cambay PSC field in India and will be assessing additional opportunities in the E&P sector.
Cambay PSC production update
Following the Company's announcement on 27 May 2026 concerning the workover program on the Cambay PSC legacy wells, the recently worked-over C-08 well has stabilised at a production level of circa 30 bopd.
Total production is currently circa 140 bopd and circa 50 MCF gas.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR") and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.
For and on behalf of Synergia Energy Limited
Roland Wessel

latest.title: Cambay PSC Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 7790F
Synergia Energy Ltd
27 May 2026
RNS Announcement
27 May 2026
AIM: SYN
Cambay PSC Update
Synergia Energy Limited (
Synergia
or the
Company
) announces the following update regarding its Cambay PSC (WI 50%) in India.
Cambay PSC Workovers
The following four legacy well workovers have been completed:
C-64: Sand build up caused the sucker rod pump ("SRP") to seize and the well was worked over to remove the sand and to optimise the SRP. The well is currently operating on a reduced pump rate and producing c. 30 bopd with a 50% water cut.
C-72: The well was re-perforated in the OSII formation and the SRP was replaced. The well is currently cleaning up the brine kill fluid.
C-19z: This well has been consistently producing small volumes of oil on natural flow. An SRP has been installed and a gas pipeline is being installed to connect gas output to the C-73 facility. Initial production rates of c. 11 bopd has since fallen to 2-3 bopd and the SRP optimisation is ongoing.
C-08: The workover operation has recently been completed and the well is currently cleaning up the brine kill fluid. The gas output is planned to be connected to the C-73 facility via a new pipeline currently under construction.
In addition to the above four wells, C-74 continues to produce c. 60 bopd and the C-77H well is producing at a rate of .08 mmscfd.
Note: All production data are gross.
C-79 new well planning
Selan and Synergia's technical teams have been in detailed discussions regarding the planned C-79 new well to be drilled into the Eocene reservoir. The discussions concern the well target which is planned to intersect the oil leg of the Eocene reservoir.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR") and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.

latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 8541D
Synergia Energy Ltd
12 May 2026
RNS Announcement
12 May 2026
AIM: SYN
Appointment of Non-Executive Director
The Board of Synergia Energy Limited (
Synergia
or the
Company
) announces the appointment of Stacey Britza as Non-Executive Director with immediate effect.
Stacey has been actively involved in the Company's activites for 18 years as Executive Assistant and Assistant Company Secretary. Consequently, Stacey brings valuable experience of the Company's activities and continuity.
Roland Wessel, Chief Executive Officer said:
"Stacey has been a valued employee of the Company for many years and her career has included Cairn Energy prior to her involvement with Oilex and Synergia. I look forward to Stacey's elevated involvement in the management of the Company."
For and on behalf of Synergia Energy Limited
Roland Wessel
CEO

latest.title: Cambay PSC and Board Changes
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 9083C
Synergia Energy Ltd
05 May 2026
RNS Announcement
05 May 2026
AIM: SYN
Cambay PSC and Board Changes
The Board of Synergia Energy Limited (
Synergia
or the
Company
) announces the following update regarding its Cambay PSC (50% WI) in India and Board structure update.
Cambay PSC Update
Oil production for April 2026 averaged 148 bopd primarily from two wells (C-64 and C-74) that were worked over in October 2025. Gas production for the month averaged 39 MCF/day from the C-77H well.
The Company is in discussion with its joint venture partner, Antelopus Selan Energy Ltd ("Selan") regarding the proposed new well (C-79) to be drilled later this year into the Eocene formation.
Board Changes
Mark Bolton, Non-Executive Director, is stepping down from the Synergia Board of Directors after several years of valuable service. Mark will continue to assist the Company under an advisory contract. The Company will look to appoint Stacey Britza, Synergia's Assistant Secretary, as a Non-Executive Director, and a further announcement will be made in due course following her appointment.
Roland Wessel, Chief Executive Officer said:
"
Mark Bolton has helped steer the Company through challenging years and we are grateful for his contribution and wish him every success for the future
."
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR") and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.

company_30s.what_they_do: SYN.L is an oil‑and‑gas exploration firm that holds a production‑sharing contract in the Cambay basin, a region known for its onshore hydrocarbon potential.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits on a potentially sizeable asset that could deliver a new supply of gas or oil to the UK market. Even modest production would give it a foothold in a sector that still values exploration upside.
company_30s.current_state: The business is still in the pre‑production phase, relying on external financing and a loan agreement to keep operations afloat. Recent filings suggest the company is divesting its main asset to Selan Exploration while adding new directors and issuing share options to shore up capital.

rns_narrative: The company has been moving its governance structure—appointing a new non‑executive director—while simultaneously raising capital through a small loan and share‑option plan.

A key theme is the divestiture of its Cambay PSC, which could provide liquidity and reduce operating risk. The sale to Selan Exploration signals a shift from exploration to a more focused, possibly cash‑generating operation.
These steps point to a company trying to stabilize its balance sheet and create a clearer path toward profitability, even if that means exiting its most valuable asset.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: silent and cautious – the community is largely watching without comment.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No bullish chatter was detected; the absence of discussion suggests investors are not yet convinced the asset sale will unlock value.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears note that selling the Cambay PSC could leave the company without a core production engine, raising doubts about future revenue streams.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone remains flat; there is no emerging optimism or panic, but the silence itself may be a contrarian signal.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is dead, meaning no recent search interest. This absence of public chatter is a classic contrarian cue for a potential surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the Cambay PSC sale to Selan Exploration, which could trigger a cash infusion and a cleaner balance sheet.
Finalisation of the £0.7m loan agreement – the release of these funds could provide working capital for drilling or marketing.
Any announcement of new drilling results or a revised production forecast that shows the PSC is closer to commercial viability.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike or a price breakout above the recent 20‑day moving average. A sustained increase in trading volume combined with a modest price lift would support a mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The company appears to be in the early stages of a potential recovery: the asset sale is the first step, followed by capital injection. If the market reacts positively to the sale, the next phase would be a gradual rebound as the company moves toward a leaner operational model.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – there is no clear signal yet, and the depth of the crash is unknown.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the asset sale proceeds and the company receives the loan funds, the market could see a repricing of the remaining PSC, potentially lifting the share price by 30–50% from its current level. A successful transition to a more focused, cash‑generating model would be the main upside.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The sale of the Cambay PSC leaves the company without a core production asset. If the company cannot secure new drilling contracts or alternative revenue streams, the share price could stay stuck or decline further.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is moderate but the downside is limited to the current price level if the company stays in a cash‑burn state. The asymmetry is small; a cautious approach is warranted.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter only after the asset sale is officially confirmed and the share price shows a 5‑10% uptick with increased volume. Size the position so that a 10% decline would only wipe out a single trade.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear volume spike (at least 30% above the 10‑day average) coupled with a price break above the 20‑day moving average. This would be a low‑risk entry point.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks, so the setup does not warrant a 'pass'.

ai_insights.bottom_line: SYN.L is a pre‑production oil‑and‑gas explorer that is divesting its main asset and raising capital; the lack of public chatter and a dead Google trend make it a neutral contrarian play until a clear catalyst—such as the sale completion—unfolds.
